channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Memphis judge maintains $1 million bond for man charged with firing shots at Jewish school
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A judge on Monday declined to reduce a $1 million bond for a man charged with shooting at a contractor at a Jewish school in Memphis, after his attorney requested a lower amount to allow him to secure his release and get proper mental health treatment outside of jail.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lisa Bonet files for divorce from Jason Momoa 18 years after they became a couple
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Lisa Bonet has filed for divorce from Jason Momoa 18 years after the two actors became a couple. The 56-year-old Bonet, whose legal name is Lilakoi Moon, filed documents to end her marriage to the 44-year-old Momoa in Los Angeles County court on Monday.
item.dc:creator: AP, KRON4

item.title: United Airlines found loose bolts, other issues on a key part of grounded Boeing 737 Max 9 jetliners
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — United Airlines said Monday it found loose bolts and other “installation issues” on a part of some Boeing 737 Max 9 jets that were inspected after a mid-flight fuselage blowout on a similar Alaska Airlines jet Friday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, DAVID KOENIG and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Moon landing attempt by U.S. company may be doomed
item.description: The first U.S. moon landing attempt in more than 50 years appeared to be doomed after a private company's spacecraft developed a “critical” fuel leak just hours after Monday's launch.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: From Tampa Bay to a National Championship: Michael Penix Jr. has been paving the road to success since high school
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- On Monday night, Tampa Bay's very own Michael Penix Jr. will lead the Washington Huskies in the 2024 College Football Playoff National Championship against the undefeated Michigan Wolverines. For Penix Jr.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Hampshire attorney general suggests national Dems broke law by calling primary 'meaningless'
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — New Hampshire’s attorney general on Monday ordered national Democratic party leaders to stop calling the state’s unsanctioned presidential primary “meaningless,” saying doing so violates state law.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michigan cosmetology school reaches $2.8M settlement in unpaid labor case
item.description: The Michigan-based Douglas J Aveda Institute, a cosmetology school, has reached a $2.8 million settlement over claims students performed unpaid labor unrelated to their field of study.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada judge attacked by defendant in viral court video finishes sentencing him
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man who leaped over a judicial bench and attacked a Nevada judge on video last week was shackled and closely watched Monday as he appeared before the same judge and was sentenced to up to four years in state prison.
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 'Multiple' people hurt after explosion at Fort Worth hotel, authorities say
item.description: Aerial footage in Fort Worth, Texas, showed debris from a hotel scattered across downtown streets Monday as authorities said they were responding to a “major incident” and urged people to avoid the area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders new North Dakota legislative district for 2 Native American tribes
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday ordered a new joint North Dakota legislative district for two Native American tribes that successfully argued a map created through redistricting in 2021 violated the Voting Rights Act by diluting their voting strength. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US secretary of state rallies Mideast leaders to prepare for Gaza's post-war future
item.description: AL ULA, Saudi Arabia (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Monday that four key Arab nations and Turkey have agreed to begin planning for the reconstruction and governance of Gaza once Israel’s war against Hamas ends.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Explosion at historic Texas hotel injures 11 and scatters debris in downtown Fort Worth
item.description: Authorities say an explosion at a historic Texas hotel in downtown Fort Worth on Monday blew out windows, littered downtown streets with large piles of debris from the building and injured 11 people, including one person who is in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Explosion at Fort Worth hotel injures at least 11 people, gas leak 'likely' cause
item.description: A Fort Worth, Texas hotel was the scene of an explosion. Photos showed major damage to the bottom floors of the hotel. Police told people to stay away from the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Americans are not satisfied with the way democracy is working
item.description: Both Republicans and Democrats are less satisfied with the way democracy is working in the U.S. compared to 2021.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republicans oust state party chairman facing rape allegations in critical election year
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The Republican Party of Florida ousted Chairman Christian Ziegler and elected his replacement in a special vote on Monday as police investigate a rape accusation against him. The turmoil was an unwelcome distraction for the state’s top Republican, Gov.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dry January: What an alcohol-free month can do for your health
item.description: Of those who participated in Dry January in 2018, 71% had improved sleep, 58% lost weight, 54% saw an improvement in their skin and 67% had more energy, according to a study.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, The Hill

item.title: Classes resume in Michigan State building where deadly shooting transpired
item.description: Berkey Hall, the Michigan State University building where two of the three victims of last year's mass shooting were killed, reopened Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California inmate's death after prison assault being investigated as homicide
item.description: Two convicted murderers are once again suspected in a homicide investigation following their alleged assault of a 29-year-old inmate at Centinela State Prison in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska Air 1282: Man finds passenger's iPhone, says it's 'perfectly' intact after falling from sky
item.description: Law enforcement officials in Washington County are urging anyone who finds potential debris from Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 to reach out to authorities after the NTSB disclosed late Saturday the blown out part of the aircraft could be around Barnes Road near Hwy 217 and the Cedar Hills neighborhood
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: United finds loose bolts on some 737 MAX 9 planes during inspections
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- United Airlines said on Monday that it found some loose bolts on several 737 MAX 9 planes during its inspections required by the Federal Aviation Administration after one of the plane's parts blew out Friday mid-air on an Alaska Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police searching for Indiana inmate who escaped work crew
item.description: Officials with the Grant County Sheriff's Office are looking for an inmate who reportedly escaped from his work-crew detail Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Jo Koy addresses NFL-Taylor Swift Golden Globes joke: 'I'm not going to let this bring me down'
item.description: Jo Koy is a trending topic just hours after stepping off the Golden Globes stage in Beverly Hills, but the comic feels some anger over Taylor Swift's expression during the award show may have been misguided.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: World is more hopeful for 2024: Report
item.description: Public sentiment on AI is divided with 56% expecting positive outcomes such as AI-assisted medical practices while 64% globally anticipate job losses.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, The Hill

item.title: Bottle washes up in France 3 years after South Carolina fisherman dropped it in sea
item.description: A South Carolina fisherman who was asked to drop a memorial bottle 50 miles off the coast in 2021 says it was recently found in France.
item.dc:creator: Adrianna Lawrence, KTLA 5

item.title: Scientists find about a quarter million invisible nanoplastic particles in a liter of bottled water
item.description: The average liter of bottled water has nearly a quarter million invisible pieces of ever so tiny nanoplastics, detected and categorized for the first time by a microscope using dual lasers.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yellen says 100,000 firms have joined a business database aimed at unmasking shell company owners
item.description: VIENNA, Va. (AP) — Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen announced that 100,000 businesses have joined a new database that collects “beneficial ownership” information on firms as part of a new government effort to unmask shell company owners.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Can Congress land a deal on Ukraine aid and border security as lawmakers return to Washington?
item.description: Senate negotiators were trying to close on a bipartisan border security proposal this week that could unlock Senate Republican support for Ukraine aid.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Headless body found in California vineyard 13 years ago identified, case still unsolved
item.description: In a stunning revelation, a decapitated body found nearly 13 years ago in a vineyard outside Arvin has been identified.
item.dc:creator: Jason Kotowski, Fox 5

item.title: 'Four more years!' chant drowns out protest at Biden speech at church in South Carolina
item.description: WASHINGTON (NEXSTAR) – President Joe Biden’s speech at the Mother Emanuel African Methodist Episcopal Church in South Carolina was interrupted by protestors calling for a ceasefire in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kounelias, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the 2024 Golden Globes: What you didn’t see on camera
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The Golden Globe Award s likes to think of itself as the party of the year, but instead of a debauched, champagne-fueled romp, it can sometimes feel more like a strange high school reunion for the extremely famous.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 'Soldiers of Christ' killing unsettles Korean Americans in Georgia and stokes fear of cults
item.description: LAWRENCEVILLE, Ga. (AP) — Just days into the admission process for Soldiers of Christ, Sehee Cho was faint and feeble. The 33-year-old had come to the Atlanta suburb of Lawrenceville from South Korea in July to heal from a traumatic experience.
item.dc:creator: SUDHIN THANAWALA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In first interview since conviction, Jonathan Majors says he hopes to work in Hollywood again
item.description: In his first interview since his assault and harassment conviction last month, actor Jonathan Majors said he hopes to work in Hollywood again. “I pray I do, but it’s God’s plan and God’s timing,” Majors said in the interview that aired Monday on ABC’s “Good Morning America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House, Pentagon will review Defense Secretary Austin's lack of disclosure on his hospital stay
item.description: Both the White House and Pentagon say they will look into why President Joe Biden and other top officials weren’t informed for days that Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin had been hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Seung Min Kim And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: United Airlines finds loose bolts, other issues on a key part of grounded Boeing 737 Max 9 jetliners
item.description: United Airlines says it has found loose bolts and other problems on a key part of its grounded Boeing 737 Max 9 jetliners.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush And David Koenig, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alaska jet's recovered door 'plug' may hold clues into inflight blowout
item.description: Investigators said Sunday they had found the piece of fuselage that blew off a Boeing airliner over Oregon on Friday, and hoped it would provide physical evidence of what went wrong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Falcons fire coach Arthur Smith hours after completing 3rd straight 7-10 season
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Arthur Smith, who inherited a rebuilding project in his first NFL head coaching job and failed to lift Atlanta from its playoff drought, was fired late Sunday night, hours after completing his third straight losing season with the Falcons.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tyre Nichols' family gathers for vigil 1 year after police brutally beat him
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — One year ago, Tyre Nichols was driving home to have dinner with his family when he was pulled over by Memphis police who claimed the Black man was driving recklessly and yanked him out of his car.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
